attacking_style: Attacks decisively on long, steady-gradient climbs (20km at ~5%), establishing dominance early on the ascent and holding power through sustained solo efforts rather than explosive accelerations.
team_tactics: Limited team visibility in observations; appears to rely on individual strength rather than team setup, launching self-sufficient solo moves.
terrain_preference: Excels on category one climbs with sustained moderate gradients (5-6%) over 20km where power-to-weight and diesel engine capabilities matter more than explosive accelerations. Unexposed on steep/punchy or high-altitude finishes in available data.
vulnerability: Documented 'early Vuelta struggles' suggest susceptibility to poor form phases or difficulties with race rhythm in opening stages of Grand Tours.
key_patterns: Establishes gaps on long climbs through sustained power rather than repeated accelerations (single observation).
Successfully defends solo advantages against organized chase groups over extended distances (1:27 margin).
Shows form recovery trajectory within single Grand Tour after documented early-race difficulties.
form_trajectory: positive: Demonstrated recovery from early Vuelta struggles to win via 20km+ solo effort, indicating strong progression within the race
